skills.pattern: The testbench tries to access `dut.clk` but the RTL module's clock port is named `PCLK`, not `clk`, causing an AttributeError.
skills.fix: Update the testbench to use the correct port name `PCLK` from the RTL module instead of the nonexistent `clk`.

skills.pattern: The testbench attempts to access a signal named `clk` on the DUT, but the RTL APB slave module defines the clock port as `PCLK`, causing an attribute error because the name does not match.
skills.fix: Rename all references from `dut.clk` to `dut.PCLK` in the testbench to match the RTL port name.

skills.pattern: The testbench references a port named "clk" but the RTL module defines the clock as "PCLK" instead.
skills.fix: Rename the port in the testbench from "clk" to match the RTL port name "PCLK".

skills.pattern: The testbench file references macros (`reference_tb`, `dut`) that are undefined in the compilation context, causing a syntax error during parsing.
skills.fix: Define the macros `reference_tb` and `dut` in the Verilog/SystemVerilog testbench or replace them with actual module instantiation/hierarchy paths.

skills.pattern: The testbench tries to access dut.clk but the RTL module has no port named "clk" — it uses "PCLK" instead.
skills.fix: Rename the clock signal reference in the testbench from "clk" to "PCLK" to match the RTL port name.

skills.pattern: The testbench references "dut.clk" but the RTL module port is named "PCLK", causing AttributeError.
skills.fix: Update all references from "dut.clk" to "dut.PCLK" in the testbench to match the actual RTL port name.

skills.pattern: The Verilog file contains backtick macro references (`reference_tb`, `apb_slave`, `dut`) that are likely intended for a SystemVerilog/Verilog testbench but are causing syntax errors when compiled as pure Verilog without the macros being defined.
skills.fix: Remove the erroneous macro reference lines at the top of apb_slave.v so that only valid Verilog module code remains.

skills.pattern: The testbench references `dut.clk` but the RTL module defines the clock port as `PCLK`, not `clk`, causing an attribute lookup failure.
skills.fix: Update all references from `dut.clk` to `dut.PCLK` in the testbench to match the RTL port name.

skills.pattern: The testbench is trying to access a signal named `clk` but the RTL module defines it as `PCLK`.
skills.fix: Rename the module port from `PCLK` to `clk` in the RTL, or update the testbench to use the correct port name `dut.PCLK`.

skills.pattern: The testbench references `dut.clk` but the RTL module port is named `PCLK`, not `clk`.
skills.fix: Rename the RTL port from `PCLK` to `clk`, or update the testbench to reference `dut.PCLK`.
